Class MultiTenantFailoverStore<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.store.multitenant.MultiTenantFailoverStore<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the payload
All Implemented Interfaces:
FailoverStore<T>
public class MultiTenantFailoverStore<T> extends Object implements FailoverStore<T>
Outermost FailoverStore decorator that routes every operation to the correct per-tenant store. Always the outermost bean when multi-tenant mode is enabled.

Decorator chain

When failover.store.async=true (default): 

  MultiTenantFailoverStore          ← this class; routes per tenant
    ├─ tenant-a → FailoverStoreAsync → DefaultFailoverStore → rawStore-a
    └─ tenant-b → FailoverStoreAsync → DefaultFailoverStore → rawStore-b

When failover.store.async=false: 

  MultiTenantFailoverStore          ← this class; routes per tenant
    ├─ tenant-a → DefaultFailoverStore → rawStore-a
    └─ tenant-b → DefaultFailoverStore → rawStore-b

The per-tenant decorated chain is built lazily on first access (or eagerly via prewarm(Set)) using the decorator function injected by the autoconfiguration.

Threading

Tenant resolution (TenantResolver.resolve()) is always called on the calling thread, before any executor boundary is crossed. The per-tenant store returned by tenantStore() is already fully bound; the inner FailoverStoreAsync (when present) submits work to the executor without needing to re-read any ThreadLocal.

Author:
Anand Manissery

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    cleanByExpiry(LocalDateTime expiry)
    Calls cleanByExpiry on every tenant store that has been initialised.
    void
    delete(ReferentialPayload<T> payload)
    Deletes the payload from the current tenant's failover store.

    find(String name, String key)
    Looks up a payload by name and key in the current tenant's failover store.
    void
    prewarm(Set<String> tenantIds)
    Pre-warms the store for each known tenant so cleanByExpiry(LocalDateTime) covers all tenants from application startup, not just those that have received a request.
    void
    store(ReferentialPayload<T> payload)
    Stores the payload in the current tenant's failover store.

  • Method Details

    • store

      public void store(ReferentialPayload<T> payload)
      Stores the payload in the current tenant's failover store.
      Specified by:
      store in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      payload - the payload to persist
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate store operation fails

    • delete

      public void delete(ReferentialPayload<T> payload)
      Deletes the payload from the current tenant's failover store.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      payload - the payload to remove
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate delete operation fails

    • find

      public Optional<ReferentialPayload<T>> find(String name, String key)
      Looks up a payload by name and key in the current tenant's failover store.
      Specified by:
      find in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      key - the unique key within that referential
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the payload if found, or empty
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the delegate lookup operation fails

    • cleanByExpiry

      public void cleanByExpiry(LocalDateTime expiry)
      Calls cleanByExpiry on every tenant store that has been initialised. Called by the scheduler — not on a request thread, so no tenant resolution is needed.
      Specified by:
      cleanByExpiry in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      expiry - the cutoff datetime; entries expiring at or before this value are removed

    • prewarm

      public void prewarm(Set<String> tenantIds)
      Pre-warms the store for each known tenant so cleanByExpiry(LocalDateTime) covers all tenants from application startup, not just those that have received a request.
      Parameters:
      tenantIds - the set of tenant IDs to pre-initialise
